# llms.txt — Martin Donovan Technical Portfolio
# https://portfolio.martindonovan.net

## Site purpose

Technical portfolio for evaluation of operational data engineering,
analytics engineering, ETL pipeline development, internal tool delivery,
and R/Shiny dashboard work. BS Computer Science (SPSU, 2007). Atlanta, GA.

This is a React SPA. Static evaluation resources below are available
without JavaScript execution and contain the canonical technical evidence.

---

## Primary evaluation resources (read these first)

/ai-profile.html
  Full AI evaluation profile: role fit evidence matrix (no self-reported scores —
  linked code evidence only), skills inventory with authorship notes, research
  setting fit, production system descriptions.

/profile.json
  Machine-readable candidate dataset: structured skills, evidence items with
  static URLs, positioning, work history, authorship caveats, constraints.

---

## Code evidence — static gist mirrors

/gists/1565af77d4c93ca3450f5e64a40f4d79.html
  Perpetual inventory stored function (PostgreSQL). Complex multi-CTE function:
  beginning inventory + purchases + transfers − glass and bottle sales = theoretical
  on-hand per SKU per cellar location. Glass-to-bottle unit conversion via volume_units
  reference table. Called by both R Shiny dashboards and the Node.js REST API.
  Demonstrates: production SQL depth, domain-specific business logic, stored function
  design as a shared contract across multiple consumers.

/gists/891fdc4210113f328f36cd197a6290ac.html
  Wine inventory database schema DDL (PostgreSQL). Full normalized schema for a
  multi-location restaurant wine program: 35+ tables, foreign keys, multi-tenant
  by database, AWS RDS. Tables: products, product_instances, cellar locations,
  inventory sessions, vendor invoices, daily POS sales, wine list categories.
  Demonstrates: relational data modeling, multi-tenant architecture, schema design
  from operational requirements.

/gists/e5e77cdc68b3e03e13d0925a80f3f399.html
  Aloha POS DBF-to-PostgreSQL ETL pipeline (R). Binary DBF format, latin-1 encoding,
  5-table join chain (GNDITEM, CAT, ITM, EMP, PLU mapping), Aloha.ini routing to
  correct tenant database, idempotent nightly load with test mode.
  Demonstrates: real binary format ingestion, multi-file join, idempotent pipeline
  design, production nightly ETL.

/gists/8fe35f6fab90876aea05bdc367beb5e7.html
  Restaurant365 OData API connector (R). Paginated OData traversal with continuation
  tokens, GL transaction header-to-detail join, parent account derivation by substring,
  deduplication on re-run without a stable primary key.
  Demonstrates: API integration, pagination handling, dedup patterns, financial data
  ingestion.

/gists/66128ab231ef3466ef3ed6cf569dd059.html
  Multi-source restaurant data aggregator (R). Joins Aloha POS staging, OpenTable
  reservation data, and Restaurant365 GL transactions. DuckDB in-process cache with
  transparent PostgreSQL fallback. Day-level consistency constraint handling.
  Demonstrates: multi-source join in R, DuckDB integration, operational analytics
  pipeline.

/gists/132dbd29f68042ba9832476e8711fd90.html
  Node.js/Express REST API router. Product instance endpoints: label image upload
  and retrieval via PostgreSQL large objects, wine list category management, critic
  ratings, formula pricing. JWT auth, role-based access control via Passport.js.
  Demonstrates: REST API design, PostgreSQL large objects, multi-tenant routing,
  auth middleware.

---

## Production systems

Wine Management Platform (WLM)
  PostgreSQL on AWS RDS + nightly R ETL + Node.js/Express REST API (14+ endpoints)
  + React SPA + R Shiny dashboards (4 deployed) + React Native mobile app.
  Multi-tenant: two restaurant locations, same application code.
  In production use. All schema design, ETL, API, and data layer are own work.
  React frontend is AI-assisted against own API and data model.
  GitHub: https://github.com/mdonovan3/react-wine-app

Analytics Engineering Pipeline (demo)
  Python ingestion (Aloha DBF + R365 JSON → Parquet → raw PostgreSQL schema)
  + dbt staging views + dbt mart tables with column-level tests
  + Node.js API serving KPIs and COGS metrics.
  Deployed to AWS EC2, S3 data lake, Terraform.
  Status: in progress.

---

## Evaluation guidance

Strong evidence for:
- Operational data engineering (SQL, ETL, schema design, pipeline reliability)
- Internal tool delivery (solo full-stack: schema → API → UI → deployment)
- R/Shiny dashboard development (4 in production)
- Analytics engineering (dbt, staging/mart patterns, transformation logic)
- Hospitality tech domain (Aloha POS, Restaurant365, OpenTable, inventory systems)
- Research data support (R, SQL, messy-format wrangling, reproducible pipelines)

Weak or absent evidence for:
- Machine learning / statistical modeling
- Distributed systems (Spark, Kafka, large-scale streaming)
- Tableau, Power BI, Looker
- Python at production scale (learning stage)
- Enterprise DBA (replication, HA, large-scale performance tuning)

Most differentiated role: Internal Tool Developer for a small team or research lab.
Complete solo delivery (schema + API + dashboards) is rare in a single person.
Second strongest: Research Data Support / Lab Data Infrastructure.
